overview: From 2026-05-30 to 2026-05-31, elizaos/eliza had 2 new PRs (0 merged), 0 new issues, and 1 active contributors.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator) + fix(ui): capability parity + sensitive-request mount + startup local-origin fallback
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary Build-out **and hardening** of the `/orchestrator` coding-agent workbench, plus a small app-shell startup-robustness fix. Continues the orchestrator-view work and **stays in lockstep with the team's parallel orchestrator commits on `develop`** — where this overlapped (the sub-agent credential bridge, the DRY capability const, the `getCodingAgentStatus` thread fold-in), I took `develop`'s canonical version and kept only what's genuinely additive. Synced to **0 behind `develop`**. The two transient `orchestrator-task-service` naming reds were `develop`'s own WIP and are resolved by the sync (the team re-pointed those assertions at the ACP spawn payload). Full verification below. ## What's in here ### Orchestrator - **`plugin-task-coordinator/src/index.ts`** — capabilities are a single shared `ORCHESTRATOR_CAPABILITIES` const reused across the GUI/XR/TUI views, with param schemas so a voice/NL planner can discover each capability's arguments (converged with `develop`). - **Capability manifest↔dispatch parity regression test** (new) — `orchestrator-capability-parity.test.ts` fails if the declared capabilities ever drift from what `runOrchestratorCapability` dispatches (this drifted before: `orchestrator-update-task`/`-validate-task`). Adds a `test` script so CI (`turbo run test --filter=./plugins/*`) actually runs it. - **`packages/app-core/src/api/server.ts`** — mount `handleSensitiveRequestRoutes` (built + unit-tested upstream); each branch self-authorizes via `ensureCallerAuthorized`, so it doesn't widen the unauthenticated surface. - **`agent-routes.ts`** — document the intentionally-empty `/metrics` and `/workspace-files` stubs; **`docs/orchestrator-buildout-followups.md`** — code-grounded status + Tier-2 roadmap. ### App-shell startup hardening (separable concern) - **`packages/ui/src/state/startup-phase-poll.ts`** — a stale `elizaos:active-server` pointing at a now-unreachable / CSP-blocked remote backend used to wedge first-run forever (the poll retried the dead address until `BACKEND_TIMEOUT`; clearing cookies didn't help — the pointer is in localStorage). Now: on a **connection-level** failure (no HTTP response) against a non-loopback, non-same-origin base on an http(s) page — **never on native mobile**, where the remote IS the agent — clear the saved server, re-point at the local origin, and reach the backend (one-shot). Decision is a pure exported `shouldFallBackToLocalOrigin`. - **Regression tests** — a unit matrix over `shouldFallBackToLocalOrigin` (fires for the wedge; never for HTTP errors / loopback / same-origin / mobile / non-http) plus an end-to-end test driving full recovery from a dead remote to `BACKEND_REACHED`. > This app-shell fix is logically separate from the orchestrator feature; happy to split it > into its own PR if reviewers prefer. It's included here because it was found and fixed in the > same first-run-hardening pass. ## Explicitly NOT duplicated Where `develop` shipped the same thing in parallel — credential-bridge source, the `getCodingAgentStatus` thread fold-in, a `getCodingAgentStatus` test, the orchestrator naming tests — this branch takes `develop`'s version. `client-agent.ts` is byte-identical to `develop`; `server.ts` differs only by an auth-safety comment; `index.ts` differs only by a biome-compliant line wrap. ## Verification - `plugin-agent-orchestrator` unit suite: **500 passed / 6 skipped** (the 2 prior naming reds resolved by the sync). - `packages/ui`: startup-fallback tests + `getCodingAgentStatus` test — **11 passed**. - `plugin-task-coordinator`: capability-parity test — **1 passed** (15 declared = 15 dispatched). - Typecheck clean across touched files (only pre-existing missing-optional-module errors remain); biome clean on every touched file. - Live (earlier): `/orchestrator` loads styled, controls work, agent responds via model. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Greptile Summary

This PR adds Cerebras as a first-party BYOK provider across the full stack (auth types, provider-switch config, vault, first-run catalog, UI account dialog), hardens the app-shell startup poll with an auto-recovery path for the "auth-required + pairing-disabled" dead end, and adds an orchestrator error-boundary so unhandled service rejections return HTTP 500 instead of leaving requests hanging. - **Cerebras provider wiring**: `cerebras-api` is registered end-to-end — `DirectAccountProvider`, `FIRST_RUN_PROVIDER_CATALOG`, `PROVIDER_DEFAULT_MODELS`, `directProviderBaseUrl`, and the vault key-to-id map. `PROVIDER_LABELS` is missing the matching `cerebras` entry, causing the raw env-var name to surface in the vault UI. - **Startup poll hardening**: `isRecoverableRemoteBase` is extracted and reused in the new auth-dead-end path. New regression tests cover fresh-user, returning-user, and pairing-enabled guard cases. - **Orchestrator error boundary**: `handleOrchestratorRoutes` wraps `dispatchOrchestratorRoutes` in a try/catch; a new regression test verifies the 500 path.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after adding the missing Cerebras label in the vault. The vault's PROVIDER_LABELS map is missing a cerebras entry despite CEREBRAS_API_KEY being added to PROVIDER_KEY_TO_ID. defaultLabel falls back to label ?? key, so the vault UI would display the raw string CEREBRAS_API_KEY rather than Cerebras for any user who stores a Cerebras key. Everything else — startup-poll recovery logic, orchestrator error boundary, Cerebras provider wiring, and escape-hatch buttons — looks correct and is well-tested. packages/vault/src/inventory.ts — the PROVIDER_LABELS map needs a cerebras entry to match the new PROVIDER_KEY_TO_ID registration.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/vault/src/inventory.ts | Replaces regex-based provider-key classification with exact-key lookup. Adds CEREBRAS_API_KEY to PROVIDER_KEY_TO_ID but omits the matching entry in PROVIDER_LABELS, causing the vault UI to show the raw env-var name instead of Cerebras. | | packages/ui/src/state/startup-phase-poll.ts | Extracts isRecoverableRemoteBase and adds auth-required+pairing-disabled dead-end recovery path. Logic and test coverage are solid; IPv6 loopback check bug was flagged in a prior review thread. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/api/orchestrator-routes.ts | Adds top-level error boundary wrapping dispatchOrchestratorRoutes; unhandled service rejections now return 500 instead of leaving requests hanging. Clean and well-tested. | | packages/agent/src/api/provider-switch-config.ts | Adds Cerebras to PROVIDER_DEFAULT_MODELS with a shared CEREBRAS_MODEL key for small/large, and extends clearPersistedFirstRunConfig to purge all provider-default model env vars on reset. | | packages/ui/src/components/shell/PairingView.tsx | Adds Use a local agent instead escape button; correctly placed inside the pairing-disabled branch so it is never shown when actual pairing is available. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[runPollingBackend loop] --> B{getAuthStatus} B -->|required=false| C[Check firstRunStatus - BACKEND_REACHED] B -->|required=true with token| D[BACKEND_REACHED firstRunComplete=true] B -->|required=true no token| E{bootstrapRequired?} E -->|yes| F[BACKEND_REACHED firstRunComplete=false] E -->|no| G{pairingEnabled=false AND isRecoverableRemoteBase?} G -->|yes| H[recoverToLocalOrigin - clear server, reset deadline] H --> A G -->|no| I[BACKEND_AUTH_REQUIRED - PairingView] I --> J[User clicks Use a local agent instead] J --> K[startFreshFirstRunReload] B -->|network error| L{shouldFallBackToLocalOrigin?} L -->|yes| H L -->|no| M[accumulate error - eventual BACKEND_TIMEOUT] ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/__tests__/unit/orchestrator-capability-parity.test.ts`, line 817-818 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/1ee907a7f8b073de53c86ff139599f79d6ba6b03/plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/__tests__/unit/orchestrator-capability-parity.test.ts#L817-L818)) **Regex silently misses single-quoted case labels** The pattern `/case\s+"(orchestrator-[a-z-]+)"/g` only captures `case "..."` with double quotes. Any capability dispatched via `case 'orchestrator-...'` (single quotes) would be treated as absent, causing the parity test to falsely report a dispatch-without-declaration violation. This is likely enforced by Biome today, but a comment noting the assumption would harden the test against future formatter changes.
Reviews (7): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(ui): add a "Start over" escape to st..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/940a01c32c906641dedc6771861ea544a90c2e58) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34764168)

topPRs.title: fix(core): resolve web lookups to a search action, never a shell fallback
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## What `findWebLookupActionName` no longer falls back to a SHELL action when no real search action is registered. It returns `undefined`, so the model answers directly (an honest "I don't have live access" decline) instead of force-routing a live-info ask to SHELL as a required tool. ## Why A shell is not a web-lookup tool. On a backend that has shell but no search action, "what's the current price of X / exchange rates / weather" was force-routed to SHELL as a **required** tool. A weak planner can't drive that, loops on the required-tool cap, and surfaces a generic "something went wrong" (and in practice the sub-agent writes throwaway fetch scripts). Returning `undefined` lets the turn decline honestly. Genuine shell requests still route via the separate `looksLikeLocalShellRequest` path — no regression there. ## Precedent This is the **action-routing-level symmetry of #7942** (`a79b1d3b5e`), which banned the SHELL fallback for chat-message search/recall at the planner-prompt level. Same principle: a shell is not a substitute for a missing search backend. ## Tests - Rewrites the 3 "legacy current-info-to-shell" stage-1 tests + 1 bogus-candidate test to the corrected contract (no search action → `direct_reply`, no forced SHELL, `shellHandler` not called). - Adds a focused unit test for `findWebLookupActionName` (SEARCH/BRAVE_SEARCH preferred; SHELL-only → `undefined`; empty → `undefined`). Full core suite green locally (1380 passed).

Greptile Summary

This PR removes the SHELL action fallback from `findWebLookupActionName`, so web/live-info lookups that find no registered search action now return `undefined` instead of routing to SHELL. The function is also exported to enable direct unit testing. - **`services/message.ts`**: `findWebLookupActionName` now only returns a search-type action (SEARCH, BRAVE_SEARCH, etc.) or `undefined`; both call sites already guard on the return value with `if (lookupAction)`, so `undefined` naturally falls through to a `direct_reply` without hitting the planner. - **Tests**: Three stage-1 tests and one bogus-candidate test are rewritten from expecting `planned_reply`+`shellHandler` to expecting `direct_reply`+no planner call; a new focused unit test for `findWebLookupActionName` is added to the live-regression suite.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the change is narrow, well-tested, and correctly handled at both call sites. The only behavioral change is that runtimes with a shell action but no search action no longer force-route web-lookup requests through the planner; they produce a clean direct_reply instead. Both call sites already check if (lookupAction) before acting on the return value, so the new undefined path is handled correctly without any code changes at the call sites. All affected test cases have been rewritten to the corrected contract. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/core/src/services/message.ts | Removes the SHELL fallback from findWebLookupActionName; exports the function so it can be unit-tested directly. Both call sites already guard on the returned value, so returning undefined safely produces a direct_reply. | | packages/core/src/__tests__/message-routing-live-regression.test.ts | Adds a focused unit test for findWebLookupActionName validating SEARCH/BRAVE_SEARCH preferred, SHELL-only returns undefined, and empty returns undefined. | | packages/core/src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts | Rewrites three shell-fallback stage-1 tests to assert direct_reply and no ACTION_PLANNER call when only a shell action is registered for a web-lookup request. | | packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/message-handler-bogus-candidates.test.ts | Updates bogus-candidate test to expect simple=true and no SHELL in candidateActions when only a synthetic action name is present and no real search backend is registered. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[Web/live-info lookup request] --> B{looksLikeLocalShellRequest?} B -- Yes --> C[findAvailableActionName SHELL aliases] C -- found --> D[return shellAction] C -- not found --> E{looksLikeWebSearchRequest?} B -- No --> E E -- Yes --> F[findWebLookupActionName] F --> G{Search action registered?\nSEARCH / BRAVE_SEARCH / etc.} G -- Yes --> H[return searchAction planned_reply] G -- No --> I[return undefined] I --> J[return empty array] J --> K[direct_reply: honest decline] E -- No --> L[other routing checks...] style I fill:#f96,color:#000 style K fill:#6a6,color:#fff style H fill:#6a6,color:#fff ``` Reviews (2): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(core): resolve web lookups to a sear..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/bfd1c056158ca4bf38aae438acab720d92c649ff) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34754356)

topPRs.title: fix(prompts): answer the current date/time/year from runtime context
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Problem The no-stale-knowledge prompt rule told the agent to decline "current/live/latest" asks when no fetch tool is available — but the runtime context **always** carries a `CURRENT_TIME` signal, so "what year is it / what's today's date" was wrongly declined as if unknowable. ## Fix A one-line **prompt exception**: date/time/year are answerable directly from the always-present `CURRENT_TIME` runtime context. No code, no regex — grounded in real system state, fully general. Net **+1/−1** in `packages/prompts`. ## Scope note An earlier draft of this PR also added a keyword-regex live-quote router (`mentionsLiveQuote`) to `looksLikeWebSearchRequest`. That was intent-classification by a hand-maintained crypto-asset list and it regressed a no-web-search backend into a hard error, so it has been **dropped**. Routing a bare "btc price" to a fetch tool is correctly left to the planner against the actually-registered actions; this PR is now purely the date/time prompt fix.

Greptile Summary

Adds a prompt-rule EXCEPTION so the agent answers date/time/year questions directly from the `CURRENT_TIME` runtime context instead of declining with "I don't have live access to the date." The actual diff is one sentence inserted into `packages/prompts/src/index.ts`; no code paths in `packages/core` are changed by this PR. - **Date/time EXCEPTION**: instructs the model to answer current-date queries from runtime context, avoiding false "no live access" responses. - **PR description vs. diff**: The title and description reference a second fix ("route bare market quotes to live search" in `looksLikeWebSearchRequest`) and a new test file, but neither appears in the diff — those changes are in the base branch already or belong to a separate PR.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — a single sentence added to a prompt rule with no runtime code changes. The change is one sentence in a prompt instruction file. The CURRENT_TIME provider is always included in CORE_RESPONSE_STATE_PROVIDERS so the model will have the signal it needs when generating replies. The EXCEPTION is narrowly scoped to date/time/year and doesn't loosen any other constraint in the rule. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/prompts/src/index.ts | Single-line prompt rule update adding a date/time EXCEPTION to the no-training-metadata rule; change is well-scoped and matches the CURRENT_TIME provider's always-on behavior in CORE_RESPONSE_STATE_PROVIDERS. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant U as User participant S1 as Stage 1 (Routing) participant S2 as Stage 2 (Response) participant CT as CURRENT_TIME Provider U->>S1: "What year is it?" Note over S1: CURRENT_TIME excluded
(STAGE1_EXTRA_PROVIDER_EXCLUSIONS) S1->>S2: "route → simple=true" CT-->>S2: "Injects date/time via
CORE_RESPONSE_STATE_PROVIDERS" Note over S2: EXCEPTION rule applies:
answer from CURRENT_TIME context S2->>U: "It's 2026." (from runtime context) U->>S1: "What's the BTC price?" Note over S1: looksLikeWebSearchRequest → true S1->>S2: "route → requiresTool=true, SEARCH action" S2->>U: "On it." (then live fetch result) ``` Reviews (4): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(prompts): answer the current date/ti..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/57e9ffaef1730ad01779dd9f4685a1c159c7e8ac) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34606857)

topPRs.title: fix(orchestrator): route build requests to the coding agent via routing context, not a keyword regex
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Problem A build request ("update the website, add some fixes") could fall through to the cron scheduler's `TASKS` simile and error, because the coding `TASKS` parent was admitted to the action catalog only when a hand-maintained keyword regex (`looksLikeTaskAgentRequest`) matched the raw text. That regex classified intent by word shape — brittle (it missed `fixes` vs `\bfix\b`, "update the website", etc.) and the wrong layer. ## Fix (structural, no keyword regex) - **`TASKS.validate` becomes a pure availability gate** — the orchestrator service is present and the request isn't a personal-lifeops to-do. *Whether* the coding parent surfaces to the planner is decided by its declared `contexts: ["tasks","code",...]` + retrieval scoring against its description/similes + the Stage-1 context router — exactly how core's own parent actions gate. - **`action-examples` drops its redundant keyword gate** — its `contextGate` already scopes the provider to the code/tasks contexts. - **A planner-prompt routing rule** teaches the context router to put build/update/fix-a-website/app/landing-page requests in the `code` context, which is the ground-truth signal the structural gate consumes. Deletes the keyword regex (and its magic proximity window) entirely — no intent classification by word shape. Net **+13/−34**. ## Test Full orchestrator suite green (385 tests). The deleted keyword-fn test is replaced by the structural gate; surfacing is exercised by the existing context-routing + retrieval tests.

Greptile Summary

This PR removes the keyword-matching gate (`looksLikeTaskAgentRequest`) that previously decided whether TASKS appeared in the LLM action catalog. The routing responsibility is shifted entirely to ElizaOS's structural context system (action `contexts`, `contextGate`, and retrieval scoring), with `validate()` now returning `true` for all messages except explicit personal life-ops patterns. - **tasks.ts `validate()`**: The keyword gate is replaced with an unconditional `return true` (after the `looksLikePersonalLifeOpsTask` guard), placing full routing authority on the LLM's context selection rather than pre-filtering. - **common.ts / task-agent-frameworks.ts**: The two diverging copies of `looksLikeTaskAgentRequest` and the underlying `TASK_AGENT_COMPLEXITY_RE` regex are deleted, eliminating the source-of-truth split that the PR targets. - **action-examples.ts**: The compact-vs-detailed example toggle is removed; the provider now always returns the detailed payload, which is safe because `contextGate` already scopes the provider to `code/tasks/agent_internal` contexts. - **prompts/src/index.ts**: The domain-routing hint is updated to explicitly route build/update/fix website and app work to the coding sub-agent and separates focus-block filtering into its own bullet.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The consolidation and prompt update are clean, but the test file cited in the PR description does not exist, leaving the changed validate() behavior without unit-test coverage. The architectural shift from keyword-gating in validate() to always returning true (with only the life-ops exclusion) is intentional and documented, and the contextGate on the provider makes the examples change safe. The main concern is that the PR description cites a test file (looks-like-task-agent-request.test.ts) that does not exist anywhere in the repository, meaning the changed validate() behavior has no unit-test coverage. plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/actions/tasks.ts — the new validate() behavior is untested; the referenced test file is absent from the repository.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/actions/tasks.ts | validate() now unconditionally returns true (after the life-ops guard), adding TASKS to the action catalog for every message when the ACP service is present; the test file cited in the PR description does not exist | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/actions/common.ts | Removes the duplicate looksLikeTaskAgentRequest export; consolidation is clean with no residual dead code | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/providers/action-examples.ts | Removes the looksLikeTaskAgentRequest gate that switched between compact and detailed examples; contextGate already restricts the provider to code/tasks/agent_internal contexts, so always returning detailed text is safe when the provider runs | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/task-agent-frameworks.ts | Removes the TASK_AGENT_COMPLEXITY_RE regex and looksLikeTaskAgentRequest function; straightforward deletion with no remaining callers | | packages/prompts/src/index.ts | Routing hint updated to explicitly direct build/update website/app/feature requests to the coding sub-agent and separates focus-block filtering into its own bullet |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[Incoming Message] --> B{ACP service present?} B -- No --> C[validate = false\nTASKS not in catalog] B -- Yes --> D{source = sub_agent?} D -- Yes --> E{Valid sub-agent metadata?} E -- Yes --> F[validate = true] E -- No --> C D -- No --> G{hasExplicitPayload?} G -- Yes --> F G -- No --> H{looksLikePersonalLifeOpsTask?} H -- Yes --> C H -- No --> F F --> I[LLM selects action via context routing] I --> J{Context = code/tasks/agent_internal?} J -- Yes --> K[codingAgentExamplesProvider returns detailed examples] J -- No --> L[Provider skipped via contextGate] ``` Reviews (7): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(orchestrator): route build requests ..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/da6d00f4027225d4985dcd90c527913a809d1eb9) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34557243)

topPRs.title: fix(core): record empty objects in trajectories as {} instead of "[object Object]"
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Problem The trajectory recorder serialized non-string values with `String()`, so objects were recorded as `[object Object]` instead of their contents, making recorded tool args/results unreadable. ## Change - Serialize object values as JSON (empty objects render as `{}`). ## Testing `bunx vitest run packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/trajectory-recorder.test.ts` — green.

Greptile Summary

Fixes a silent data corruption bug in `sanitizeForRecord` where empty plain objects were serialized as `"[object Object]"` instead of `{}`, making recorded tool call arguments and empty JSON-schema `properties` maps unreadable in trajectory files. The comment also correctly notes a latent crash: `String(Object.create(null))` throws a `TypeError` because null-prototype objects have no `toString`, so this one-line change is also a crash fix for that edge case. - **`trajectory-recorder.ts`**: Replaces `return String(value)` with `return {}` in the empty-object branch of `sanitizeForRecord`; the surrounding `seen.delete(value)` / `seen.add(value)` bookkeeping is unchanged. - **`trajectory-recorder.test.ts`**: Adds a full start→record→end→load regression test asserting that `toolCalls[0].args` and `tools[0].parameters.properties` survive the round-trip as `{}`, not `"[object Object]"`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the change is a one-line targeted fix with a direct regression test, and it cannot regress existing behavior for non-empty objects, primitives, or other value types. The fix touches exactly one branch of `sanitizeForRecord`, replacing a clearly wrong `String(value)` call (which produced `"[object Object]"` or threw for null-prototype objects) with a plain `return {}`. All other branches are untouched. The regression test exercises the full recorder lifecycle and confirms the value round-trips correctly through JSON serialization. No files require special attention.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/core/src/runtime/trajectory-recorder.ts | Single-line fix in `sanitizeForRecord`: returns `{}` for empty plain objects instead of `String(value)`, which produced `"[object Object]"` and could throw for `Object.create(null)` instances. | | packages/core/src/runtime/__tests__/trajectory-recorder.test.ts | Adds a regression test verifying that empty-object tool args and empty schema `properties` maps round-trip as `{}` rather than `"[object Object]"` after a full start/record/end/load cycle. |

Flowchart

```mermaid %%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%% flowchart TD A[sanitizeForRecord value] --> B{typeof value is object?} B -- No --> C[Handle primitives and special types] B -- Yes --> D{seen has value?} D -- Yes --> E[return Circular] D -- No --> F[seen.add value] F --> G{Object.entries empty?} G -- Yes --> H[seen.delete value] H --> I_new["return {} - FIXED"] I_old["was: String(value) = '[object Object]' or TypeError"] -.-> H G -- No --> J[Build output record with keys] J --> K[seen.delete value] K --> L[return output] ``` Reviews (5): Last reviewed commit: ["test(core): drop redundant RecordedStage..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/e66b3b00743d465936b82e71ae09e2703c6b51b2) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34557226)

topContributors.summary: NubsCarson: Drove significant architectural improvements and feature parity in elizaos/eliza through the successful merge of PR #8066, while continuing to address core stability with an open fix for web lookup actions in PR #8067. Their work today spanned 357 files, reflecting a balanced focus across feature development, bug fixes, and refactoring efforts.
